body.title: Create competition schema for classifier training
body.result: competition.memories + competition.memory_embeddings created in Postgres; holdout column included; 4 tests pass (a45300c3)
body.intention: I intend that a clean, isolated competition schema exists in Postgres that mirrors clan but has zero production data.
body.description:
Create a `competition` Postgres schema with `competition.memories` and `competition.memory_embeddings` tables mirroring the clan schema. This is the clean isolated environment for the inference competition — no production noise.

**Affected files:** New script `lab/competition/setup_competition_schema.py`
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres only
**Scope boundary:** Schema + tables only. No data. No application wiring changes.
**Completion criteria:** `psql \dt competition.*` shows `competition.memories` and `competition.memory_embeddings`; tables accept inserts matching clan schema shape.
